rejected_candidates.title: Receive-side callbacks spend nominal amounts instead of actual received balances
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported for the ZEVM handlers because `GatewayZEVM.depositAndCall` mints/deposits the exact ZRC20 amount to the target before `onCall`; the `GatewaySend` portion is also masked by F-026 because authenticated deliveries fully revert at the ABI boundary.

rejected_candidates.title: Native-input withdrawals can bypass platform fees during swap execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of F-004, which already captures the native-input fee-accounting bypass and full-`msg.value` swap spend on `withdrawToNativeChain`.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend silently treats failed ERC20 payouts as successful
rejected_candidates.reason: Masked by F-026: `GatewaySend.onCall` currently reverts at the gateway ABI boundary, so silent ERC20 `transfer` false-returns cannot persist in the deployed flow.

rejected_candidates.title: ETH payouts use 2300-gas transfer and can DoS contract recipients
rejected_candidates.reason: Masked by F-026: the `GatewaySend.onCall` path is already bricked, so this payout behavior is not independently reachable in the current code.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend onCall allows arbitrary token amount from user-provided allowance
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect root cause: `GatewaySend.onCall` pulls from the gateway, not from end users, and only receives whatever allowance/balance the gateway execution provides; the path is also already bricked by F-026.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend externalId uses block.timestamp which can be manipulated by validators
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable: `externalId` also includes contract address, sender, and a monotonic nonce, so minor timestamp skew does not create a realistic collision or theft path.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative.withdraw lacks access control and is publicly callable
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of F-008, which already keeps the only supported exploit condition: abuse of the public `withdraw` entrypoint when residual gateway allowance remains.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative claimRefund reads storage after delete risking zero amount
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of F-025; incorporated by adding `opencode_1` as supporting source for the existing event-integrity finding.
